messages.content: I'm working on a cinematic intro for my dark fantasy game, similar to Dark Souls 3. The main plot is that two people sneak into a cathedral while someone is praying to the goddess. When she appears, they assassinate her and become immortal. But not only do they become immortal, the dead also rise and can no longer die. I would like to have a text that explains this cryptically in a fairy tale/saga like way. It should be very short, please.

In an age of whispers and shadows, where dreams clutched the heart of night, there stood an ancient cathedral, shrouded in sacred silence. In its embrace, a solitary supplicant offered fervent prayers to the goddess, guardian of life's delicate dance.

But lo, from the shroud of darkness crept two silent specters, souls entwined by fate's cruel snare. With hearts eclipsed and desperate hunger for eternity's embrace, they sought to sever the divine thread that bound life to mortality.

And so, under the gaze of hallowed arches, the goddess fell, silver light dimming to a ghostly pallor. Her final breath, a whispered curse, danced upon their victory, a gift ensnared by iron irony.

For as her essence waned, a deathless tide rose. Boundless and bold, the restless dead stirred, their eternal slumber shattered. In this grim ballet, all would share the sinister kiss of immortality, a world cursed to endure, endlessly.

And thus, the saga began, of a realm forsaken by the natural order, where neither life's embrace nor death's reprieve could claim dominion ever again.

Can you make this poem more complex please. 
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
In sacred silence, a goddess’s end was wrought,
And with her demise, eternal life was bought.

Within the veiled and hallowed halls of lore,
Doth murmur ancient sagas, steeped in night's core.
Two specters cloaked in treachery's sable shroud,
Advanced with hearts where once honor proudly bowed.

'Neath sacred stillness, in clandestine refrain,
They forged the goddess's twilight, her sovereignty slain.
From such a fateful act, a grim bounty they sought,
For in her quietus, undying existence was fraught.

As whispered ripples disturbed the tranquil deep,
The once-silent sepulchers refused their hushed keep.
A relentless revenant army birthed from her cries,
Doomed to wander 'neath cold, unending skies.

Thus, in their hunger for life's unceasing flame,
The assassins conjured a world that death could not tame.
So unfolds the enigma, a ballad of immortal blight,
Forged by betrayal, beneath an everlasting night.

Can you make this poem more complex please. But it should still only have 6 sentances and they dont have to ryhme. Thanks
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
In sacred silence, a goddess’s end was wrought,
And with her demise, eternal life was bought.

Hushed lore murmurs a narrative cloaked in dusk's enigmatic embrace,
Where two spirited entities, veiled in the dark tapestry of deceit, enact their dire intent.
Amidst the sanctified hush, a divine entity succumbs to a fate meticulously orchestrated in shadow,
Her demise, a profound paradox, unfurls the crimson thread of unending existence.
With the fall of celestial grace, the abrupt severance from mortality's lineage was bartered,
An eldritch pact sealed within the silent stones, binding the living to an eternal, haunted twilight.

Can you make this poem not rhyme but also more mysterious. But it should still only have 6 sentances and they dont have to ryhme. Thanks
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
In sacred silence, a goddess’s end was wrought,
And with her demise, eternal life was bought.

In the shadow of tales long spoken in hushed tones, there unfolds the chronicle of an uncharted twilight.
Two figures, enshrouded in the mantle of treachery, dared the unthinkable beneath the cloak of obscurity.
A goddess, enshrined in sanctity's repose, met an end not foreseen, as silent vows were broken.
Her fall, a conundrum braided with the threads of infinity, bore a cursed boon beyond reckoning.
From this act, arose a chalice brimming with undying essence, a prize claimed in quietus.
And so it was that death's dominion wavered, giving way to an immortal dusk that bound soul to the undying firmament.

Can you make this poem not rhyme but also more mysterious. But it should still only have 6 sentances and they should not be much more longer. Thanks
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
In sacred silence, a goddess’s end was wrought,
And with her demise, eternal life was bought.

Whispered legends speak of an opaque tale, a nocturne shrouded in enigma.
Two defiant silhouettes emerged, swathed in the deceit of ambition's shadow.
In a sanctum wrapped in stillness, the deity met a clandestine fate.
Her undoing unfurled a tapestry of endless days, a dark gift from time's abyss.
Eternal breath was drawn from the well of her descent, a whispered exchange amidst the hallowed.
Thus, through veiled treachery, immortality bloomed, cradled in the arms of perpetual twilight.

Can you make this poem not rhyme but sound more like a fairy. But it should still only have 6 sentances and they should not be much more longer. Thanks
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
In sacred silence, a goddess’s end was wrought,
And with her demise, eternal life was bought.

Legends, softly spoken, weave a tale draped in the velvet of night.
Two daring spirits rose, shadowed by the guise of deception.
A silent sanctum bore witness to the twilight of a goddess.
Her last breath unfurled the enchantment of undying time.
Through her fall, the eternal dance was set into motion, a fateful purchase from the beyond.
And so, immortality bloomed in whispered twilight, cradled by the sacrilege of their act.

Can you make this poem not rhyme but sound more like a fairy. But it should still only have 6 sentances and they should not be much more longer. 
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
A goddess slain, an oath they swore,
Mortality bound, forevermore.

In the hush of twilight, a fable is breathed, carrying secrets of the night.
A pair of brave souls, veiled in the shroud of betrayal, emerge from the shadows.
The end of a goddess, they dared to seal, as the silent stars bore witness.
An eternal pact was inked in the stillness, their destiny entwined with the divine.
Mortality's chains, unfastened by their deed, released the essence of time unending.
Thus, in the soft glow of moonlight, forever was embraced, whispered on the lips of eternity.

Can you make this sound like a old story.
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
A goddess slain, an oath they swore,
Mortality bound, forevermore.

In the time-worn pages of lore, a tale is whispered through the shroud of countless nights.
Behold the story of two valiant souls, enshrouded in treachery's darkling weave.
Upon the altar of silence, they struck down a goddess, a vow forged in her fading breath.
Chains of everlasting life were thus wrought, in the solemn echo of their deed.
Bound to the wheel of eternity were they, and all that drew breath in the shadow of their act.
So it has been told, through the ages old, of immortality's heavy, unyielding door.

Can you make this sound like a old story. It should be very simple and only have 4 sentances
Whispered legends tell a tale of night,
Of two souls bold, cloaked in betrayal’s sight.
A goddess slain, an oath they swore,
Mortality bound, forevermore.

Ancient whispers recount a night draped in mystery, where courage and deceit danced in the dark.
Two souls, enshrouded in betrayal's cloak, dared to challenge divinity itself.
A hushed blade fell, a goddess whispered goodbye, and a solemn vow was etched into eternity.
Forevermore, mortality cast aside, the price of their sin woven into the fabric of time.
